programs.name: A Journey from Acrylics to Oils
programs.description: This introductory–intermediate course is for beginners and experienced painters who want to explore oil painting using water-mixable oils. Often seen as intimidating or toxic, oil painting becomes safe and accessible in this class. Students will learn layering, glazing, and composition while building confidence and personal expression.

programs.name: Abstract & Surreal Landscapes
programs.description: Move  beyond traditional depictions of nature and explore more abstract, expressive  approaches to landscape. Through painting, drawing, and sketching, discover how  colour, shape and composition can capture the spirit and emotion of a place  rather than its literal form. Experiment with layering, mark-making and dynamic  brushwork to translate the emotional and atmospheric qualities of the natural  world into an abstract visual language.

programs.name: Abstract Acrylic Painting: Composition
programs.description: Express yourself with paint with a focus on abstract composition. Experiment with novel ways of applying acrylic paint on canvas, such as squeegee paint, drip and splatter, flow painting and mixed media collage. Delve into the history of abstract painting, and the elements and principles of composition. Beginners and Intermediate welcome. Materials list will be provided.

programs.name: Cooking: Skills (Women)
programs.description: Cutting, chopping, searing and seasoning are just some of the skills on the menu in this activity. Come learn the basics of cooking and how to prepare delicious and nutritious recipes inspired by Canada's Food Guide. From measuring and mixing ingredients to food safety, you will gain confidence in the kitchen. The City of Toronto and its staff cannot guarantee that the foods or supplies within its cooking programs are free of allergens. Notify staff at your location of any food allergies or dietary restrictions you may have.

programs.name: Dance: Acro
programs.description: Acro is a combination of classic dance techniques and gymnastic elements. Learn introductory movements from both styles including cartwheels, elbow stands, jumps and steps through various exercises and engaging choreography.

programs.name: Dance: Caribbean
programs.description: Let rhythm and music move you with Caribbean dance! Focusing on fluidity and expression, learn Soca and jazz-style movements through a variety of exercises and engaging choreography.

programs.name: Drawing and Painting
programs.description: Explore your creativity through pencils, pastels, acrylic paint and watercolours. Learn about and practice skills including sketching, shading, brush strokes, use of colour and texture.

programs.name: Karate - Beginner
programs.description: Learn the fundamentals of competition and perform simple sparring skills while demonstrating exemplary etiquette. Participants in this program should be able to perform fundamental martial art skills and martial-art specific skills.

programs.name: Music: Guitar
programs.description: Fun and fundamentals shape this introduction to guitar. Here you will learn proper hold, tuning, strumming and chords. As you continue to practice and learn how to read music, you will work towards performing simple pieces.Equipment: You must bring your own guitar.

programs.name: Music: Guitar - Private
programs.description: Would you benefit from private music lessons? This activity provides personal instruction on the guitar with a one-on-one ratio. Here you will learn proper hold, tuning, strumming and chords.Equipment: You must bring your own guitar.

programs.name: Music: Keyboard
programs.description: Fun and fundamentals shape this introduction to keyboarding. Here you will learn proper hand positions, basic scales and how to read notes. As you continue to practice and learn how to read music you will work towards performing simple pieces.

programs.name: Music: Piano - Private
programs.description: Would you benefit from private music lessons? This activity provides personal instruction on the piano with a one-on-one ratio. Here you will learn proper hand positions, basic scales and how to read notes.

programs.name: Painting
programs.description: Create pictures with paint. Learn how to mix and choose colours, prepare surfaces, and how to use tools like sponges, brushes, stamps and even your fingers to create different textures and effects.

programs.name: Painting: Mixed Media
programs.description: Create pictures with a variety of paint types. From oil and acrylic to tempera and watercolours, learn how and when to use different paints to create specific effects.

programs.name: Pickleball: Recreational
programs.description: Hit the court for non-stop pickleball play. Whether you are a new or experienced player, want to play often or occasionally, pickleball offers something for everyone. Though not formally structured, guidelines are used to ensure participation is balanced. There is no formal instruction. Supervision, use of equipment and capacity limits vary by location, please check ahead.

programs.description: Jump in and get your feet wet. Learn how to enter and exit the pool, go underwater, breathe effectively and float with assistance.

programs.name: Preschool Swim 3: Otter
programs.description: Jump in and get your feet wet. Develop front and back swim. Learn side glides, sculling and staying afloat in deep water. Work towards a continuous swim of five metres.Prerequisite: Successful completion of or skill ability equal to Guardian Swim 2.
programs.location: Gus Ryder Pool And Health Club

programs.name: Preschool Swim 4: Dolphin
programs.description: Jump in and get your feet wet. Develop front and back swim and sidekick. Learn how to jump into deep water. Work towards a continuous swim of 10 metres.Prerequisite: Successful completion of or skill ability equal to Preschool Swim 3.

programs.name: Preschool Swim 4: Dolphin
programs.description: Jump in and get your feet wet. Develop front and back swim and sidekick. Learn how to jump into deep water. Work towards a continuous swim of 10 metres.Prerequisite: Successful completion of or skill ability equal to Preschool Swim 3.

programs.name: Stroke Improvement - Small Group
programs.description: Work on stroke correction and improving technique through drills and progressions focused on front crawl, back crawl, elementary backstroke and breaststroke. Class size is limited to four participants.Prerequisite: Successful completion of or skill ability equal to Ultra Swim 5.

programs.name: Tae Kwon Do - Beginner
programs.description: This activity introduces you to martial arts.  Here you will develop fundamental martial art skills, simple sparring skills, tactical concepts, show willingness to engage in a variety of sport activities and have confidence to try new skills.

programs.name: Tennis: Instructional
programs.description: Instructional sports activities are designed to introduce sport-specific skills while improving fundamental movement with agility, balance and coordination. programs.name: A Journey from Acrylics to Oils
programs.description: This introductory–intermediate course is for beginners and experienced painters who want to explore oil painting using water-mixable oils. Often seen as intimidating or toxic, oil painting becomes safe and accessible in this class. Students will learn layering, glazing, and composition while building confidence and personal expression.

programs.name: Abstract & Surreal Landscapes
programs.description: Move  beyond traditional depictions of nature and explore more abstract, expressive  approaches to landscape. Through painting, drawing, and sketching, discover how  colour, shape and composition can capture the spirit and emotion of a place  rather than its literal form. Experiment with layering, mark-making and dynamic  brushwork to translate the emotional and atmospheric qualities of the natural  world into an abstract visual language.

programs.name: Abstract Acrylic Painting: Composition
programs.description: Express yourself with paint with a focus on abstract composition. Experiment with novel ways of applying acrylic paint on canvas, such as squeegee paint, drip and splatter, flow painting and mixed media collage. Delve into the history of abstract painting, and the elements and principles of composition. Beginners and Intermediate welcome. Materials list will be provided.

programs.name: Cooking: Skills (Women)
programs.description: Cutting, chopping, searing and seasoning are just some of the skills on the menu in this activity. The City of Toronto and its staff cannot guarantee that the foods or supplies within its cooking programs are free of allergens. programs.name: Dance: Acro
programs.description: Acro is a combination of classic dance techniques and gymnastic elements. Learn introductory movements from both styles including cartwheels, elbow stands, jumps and steps through various exercises and engaging choreography.

programs.name: Dance: Caribbean
programs.description: Let rhythm and music move you with Caribbean dance! Focusing on fluidity and expression, learn Soca and jazz-style movements through a variety of exercises and engaging choreography.

programs.name: Drawing and Painting
programs.description: Explore your creativity through pencils, pastels, acrylic paint and watercolours. Learn about and practice skills including sketching, shading, brush strokes, use of colour and texture.

programs.name: Karate - Beginner
programs.description: Learn the fundamentals of competition and perform simple sparring skills while demonstrating exemplary etiquette. Participants in this program should be able to perform fundamental martial art skills and martial-art specific skills.

programs.name: Music: Guitar
programs.description: Fun and fundamentals shape this introduction to guitar. Here you will learn proper hold, tuning, strumming and chords. As you continue to practice and learn how to read music, you will work towards performing simple pieces.Equipment: You must bring your own guitar.

programs.name: Music: Guitar - Private
programs.description: Would you benefit from private music lessons? This activity provides personal instruction on the guitar with a one-on-one ratio. Here you will learn proper hold, tuning, strumming and chords.Equipment: You must bring your own guitar.

programs.name: Music: Keyboard
programs.description: Fun and fundamentals shape this introduction to keyboarding. Here you will learn proper hand positions, basic scales and how to read notes. As you continue to practice and learn how to read music you will work towards performing simple pieces.

programs.name: Music: Piano - Private
programs.description: Would you benefit from private music lessons? This activity provides personal instruction on the piano with a one-on-one ratio. Here you will learn proper hand positions, basic scales and how to read notes.

programs.name: Painting
programs.description: Create pictures with paint. Learn how to mix and choose colours, prepare surfaces, and how to use tools like sponges, brushes, stamps and even your fingers to create different textures and effects.

programs.name: Painting: Mixed Media
programs.description: Create pictures with a variety of paint types. From oil and acrylic to tempera and watercolours, learn how and when to use different paints to create specific effects.

programs.name: Cooking
programs.description: Let’s get creative in the kitchen and have some fun. programs.name: Cooking: Creative Chefs with Caregiver
programs.description: Small cooks with big imaginations will have the chance to take over the kitchen. A caregiver must actively participate in this activity.The City of Toronto and its staff cannot guarantee that the foods or supplies within its cooking programs are free of allergens. Notify staff at your location of any food allergies or dietary restrictions you may have.

programs.name: Make-Up - Beginner (2SLGBTQ+) - Pilot
programs.description: An introduction to  basic skin care, preparation, and essential make-up application techniques, ideal  for new or beginner participants. This class focuses on creating a natural  look, including foundation matching, blending basics, and simple mascara and  lipstick application. This program is ideal for participants who are new to  make-up application and want to build confidence with foundational techniques.    Participant  Requirements: Participants are encouraged to bring personal  foundation/concealer, eye shadow, lipstick, mascara, eyeliner, and brush sets  and other products that they already own.

programs.name: Martial Arts - Beginner

programs.name: Multi Arts
programs.description: Multi Arts looks to explore the world of Art. Here you will work with a variety of materials and mediums to learn and practice different styles and techniques.
